query: How frequently does the CDC issue new Travel Health Notices for North American countries (Mexico, Canada, Caribbean, Central America)? List recent CDC travel health notices issued in 2025 and 2026 for North American countries with their issue dates. What is the typical monthly rate of new notices affecting North America?
answer:
I've completed the research on CDC Travel Health Notices for North American countries. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Frequency & Monthly Rate
- **North America-specific new notices** (single-country/sub-region): roughly **0.25–0.5 per month** — about one new notice every 2–4 months.
- **Including global/multi-region notices** that affect North America (Global Measles, Global Dengue, Oropouche in the Americas, screwworm guidance): roughly **0.5–1 per month**.
- CDC maintains ~20–30 active notices worldwide at any given time.

## Recent 2025–2026 North America Notices
| Notice | Date | Region |
|---|---|---|
| Diphtheria in Haiti | New June 25, 2026 | Caribbean |
| Hepatitis A in Canada | New June 4, 2026 | Manitoba, Canada |
| RMSF in Mexico | Dec 23, 2025 | N. Mexico border states |
| Chikungunya in Cuba | Active late 2025 (since removed) | Caribbean |
| Oropouche in the Americas | Sept 18, 2025 | Cuba, Panama |
| Global Measles | May 28, 2025 | Canada, Mexico, Caribbean, Central America |
| Global Dengue update | May 2025 | Mexico + ~19 countries |
| Salmonella Newport in Mexico | May 30, 2024 (pre-existing) | Mexico |

## Key Patterns
- The **Caribbean and Central America** generate more notice activity than Canada or mainland Mexico, driven by mosquito-borne diseases (dengue, chikungunya, Oropouche) that surge seasonally.
- 2025–2026 activity was elevated due to record dengue in the Americas, the New World screwworm re-emergence moving north through Central America into Mexico, and a global measles resurgence.
- Notices are dynamic — added, updated, and removed over time (e.g., Chikungunya in Cuba was posted then removed).

**Important caveat:** Whether one counts only single-country North American notices vs. global notices that *include* North American destinations substantially changes the rate. The full structured findings, sources, and data points have been recorded.

sources.title: Global Dengue - Level 1 - Travel Health Notices | CDC
sources.snippet: This THN was originally posted June 20, 2024 and is updated regularly; dengue is year-round in many parts of the world with outbreaks every 2-5 years.

sources.title: Removed: Chikungunya in Cuba - Level 2 - Travel Health Notices | CDC
sources.snippet: This notice has been removed. Page last reviewed April 16, 2026.

sources.title: CDC Issues Urgent Advisory As Dengue Fever Cases Surge In Mexico And 19 Other Countries
sources.snippet: Updated in May 2025, CDC dengue travel advisory for Mexico and 19 other countries; over 13 million dengue cases across North, Central, and South America.

sources.title: New World Screwworm: Outbreak Moves into Northern Mexico | HAN | CDC
sources.snippet: CDC HAN Health Advisory CDCHAN-00526 issued January 20, 2026 regarding New World screwworm animal cases in Tamaulipas, Mexico.

sources.title: Mexico - Traveler view | Travelers' Health | CDC
sources.snippet: New World screwworm myiasis reported in Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, and Panama.

sources.title: Explosive global measles outbreaks pose risk to Canadian travellers
sources.snippet: Global measles spread continues in 2026 including explosive outbreaks in Mexico and the southern U.S.

sources.title: Measles Cases and Outbreaks | Measles (Rubeola) | CDC
sources.snippet: As of July 2, 2026, 2,170 confirmed measles cases reported in the United States in 2026.

sources.title: Global Measles - Level 1 - Travel Health Notices | CDC
sources.snippet: Global Measles THN lists countries with confirmed outbreaks including North American destinations.
